summary: Small rectangular single-story building with a stepped brick hip-like roof, a continuous band of high windows around the upper wall, stone corner pilasters, and a raised white trim/ledge surrounding the base. The walls appear to be a lower wooden plinth, a middle sandstone band, glass windows above, and a white top cornice under the roof.

shape.overall_form: rectangular footprint with concentric raised border; roof is stepped inward in tiers forming a low hip/pyramid shape
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (appears symmetric on both short ends and mirrored along center)

materials.name: quartz (or white stone)
materials.role: top cornice / surrounding trim / base border

elements: continuous horizontal row of windows (three or more per long side)
stepped brick roof with slabs at tier edges
raised white border/ledge around building footprint
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular sandstone wall ring (~11x19 internal wall area suggested) on top of a 1-block-high wooden plinth.
Place a continuous band of glass windows one block high set into the upper half of the walls; cap the walls with a white stone/quartz cornice one block thick that projects outward by one.
Add stone corner pilasters 1x1 wide, spanning from base up to the cornice at each corner (and at middle of short sides if visible).
Construct a stepped brick roof: multiple concentric brick layers stepped inward by one block per tier; add stone slabs on exposed tier corners/edges.
Surround the building with a raised white border/ledge one block high and one block away from the walls (visible outer frame).
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white trim could be quartz blocks or another white stone (uncertain).
Lower skirting appears wooden but could be a different brown planked material; exact wood type unknown.
Corner vertical blocks appear as darker stone (stonebrick vs andesite or polished stone) — exact material uncertain.
Internal layout and entrance location are not visible; assumption is single open interior.
Exact footprint dimensions estimated from images; actual block counts may differ by a few blocks.
